title: Fueling test suggests imminent debut of China’s reusable Long March 10B rocket
summary: China conducted a wet dress rehearsal for its reusable Long March 10B rocket, indicating a potential launch within weeks. This test marks a significant step toward the rocket's debut.

title: Gravitics targets 2027 flight test for ‘orbital carrier’ architecture
summary: Gravitics, a Seattle-based startup, plans to conduct a flight test in 2027 for its 'orbital carrier' architecture, which aims to pre-position spacecraft in orbit for rapid deployment.

title: Near Real-Time Payload Data Delivery with SSC Space Go
summary: SSC Space Go enables near real-time payload data delivery, highlighting the importance of ground communications in the growing commercial space market.

title: Citra Space raises $15 million Series A to expand platform for identifying objects in orbit
summary: Citra Space secured $15 million in a Series A funding round led by Washington Harbour Partners to expand its platform for tracking objects in orbit. The investment will support the company's efforts to enhance space situational awareness.

title: Atomic-6 unveils online marketplace for orbital data centers
summary: Atomic-6 has launched ODC.space, an online marketplace designed to simplify the procurement of satellites for on-orbit computing. This initiative aims to streamline access to orbital data center services.

title: Orbit is filling up fast. Now comes the awkward bit: pre-empting and handling a crisis.
summary: Earth's orbit is rapidly filling with spacecraft, risking a potential catastrophe according to experts. The situation demands urgent pre-emptive measures to manage the growing space debris crisis.

title: Key Senate appropriator rejects proposed NASA budget cuts
summary: The chairman of the Senate appropriations subcommittee that funds NASA opposes proposed budget cuts and aims to fund the agency at 2026 levels.

title: Q&A: Building a broadband constellation for a contested space era
summary: A former NASA Branch Chief and Google executive is launching a broadband constellation in LEO, focusing on narrow beams and higher performance amid growing competition in the space industry.

title: Sophia and Kepler to marry orbital compute with optical links
summary: Sophia Space and Kepler Communications announced a strategic partnership to deploy edge compute nodes on Kepler's satellites starting in late 2026. The collaboration aims to demonstrate Sophia's Orbital Data Center software using Kepler's optical data relay network for resilient space-based computing infrastructure.

title: Rocket Lab wins contract for three more iQPS launches
summary: Rocket Lab secured a contract with Japanese radar satellite company iQPS for three additional Electron launches. This expands their ongoing partnership in deploying radar imaging satellites.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts celebrate successful return to Earth | Space photo of the day for April 13, 2026
summary: Artemis 2 astronauts Victor Glover and Christina Koch celebrated their successful return to Earth after splashdown. The event was highlighted as the Space photo of the day for April 13, 2026.

title: Artemis 2: Our favorite photos from NASA's historic moon mission
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission has released stunning photos from its historic moon mission. The images showcase key moments and views from the upcoming lunar journey.

title: 'God of Chaos' asteroid Apophis will blaze across the sky on April 13, 2029 — here's why this once-in-a-lifetime event is worth traveling for
summary: The asteroid Apophis will pass extremely close to Earth on April 13, 2029, closer than some satellites. This rare event is considered a once-in-a-lifetime stargazing opportunity.

title: A worst-case solar storm could knock out satellites, GPS and power grids, report warns
summary: A new report warns that a worst-case solar storm could disrupt satellites, GPS, and power grids, potentially crippling modern technology-dependent society. Scientists emphasize the risks posed by a once-in-a-century solar event.

title: 'The most special thing that will ever happen in my life': Artemis 2 astronauts describe their epic moon mission
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts have returned to Earth after their historic moon mission, though they struggle to describe the experience. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 205 — All About Artemis
summary: Episode 205 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast covers a detailed discussion about the Artemis 2 mission by hosts Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik.

title: SpaceX launches huge 'Cygnus XL' cargo ship carrying over 5 tons of supplies to ISS astronauts (video)
summary: SpaceX launched Northrop Grumman's second 'Cygnus XL' cargo ship carrying over 5 tons of supplies to the International Space Station. The mission marks a significant milestone in commercial resupply efforts.

title: Artemis 3 and beyond: What's next for NASA after Artemis 2 moon success
summary: NASA is not stopping after the Artemis 2 moon mission success and has ambitious plans for future lunar missions, including Artemis 3 and beyond. The agency is focusing on continued exploration and development of the Moon as part of its long-term goals.

title: Splashdown! Artemis 2 astronauts return to Earth after historic NASA mission to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts successfully returned to Earth on April 10, completing a historic mission around the moon. The mission set new spaceflight records and paved the way for future lunar exploration.

title: NASA Night-light Imagery Tracks US Energy Transition, Global Volatility
summary: NASA's new nighttime satellite imagery reveals intensified artificial brightening and dimming globally, highlighting increased flaring over US oil and gas fields and changes in rural electrification and energy conservation.

title: Hubble Spies an Active Spiral
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the barred spiral galaxy IC 486, located 380 million light-years from Earth in the constellation Gemini. The galaxy is classified as a barred spiral due to its distinct central bar-shaped structure.

title: La NASA da la bienvenida a la Tierra a los exploradores lunares de Artemis II, quienes batieron récords
summary: Los astronautas de la misión Artemis II de la NASA regresaron a la Tierra tras batir récords en su vuelo de prueba a la Luna, el primero en más de 50 años. La tripulación, compuesta por Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch y Jeremy Hansen, completó con éxito la misión.

title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch Aboard Northrop Grumman CRS-24
summary: NASA launched the Northrop Grumman CRS-24 mission, sending 11,000 pounds of cargo to the International Space Station aboard the Cygnus XL spacecraft. The resupply mission includes science experiments, technology demonstrations, and crew supplies.

title: Artemis II Splashes Down
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully splashed down in the Pacific Ocean on April 10, 2026, after a nearly 10-day journey that set a record for the farthest crewed spaceflight. The mission also captured unprecedented views of the Moon's far side.

title: NASA Welcomes Record-Setting Artemis II Moonfarers Back to Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen, successfully splashed down off the coast of Earth after a historic Moon flyby. This marked the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: New Perspective of Home
summary: Artemis II's lunar flyby on April 6, 2026, will capture a unique perspective of Earth and the Moon aligned in the same frame. The Moon's surface will appear in sharp detail with Earth in the background, softly lit by the Sun.

title: Earthset From the Lunar Far Side
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew captured stunning images of Earth from the far side of the Moon during their mission. This marks a significant milestone in lunar exploration and human spaceflight.

title: Indoor Testing Facilities available at the NASA Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC)
summary: NASA's Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC) has introduced a WindShaper fan array for dynamic low-speed and hovering flight research, capable of generating arbitrary wind gradients and gusts via a Python API. The facility also offers a WindProbe for flow surveys using the lab's OptiTrack motion capture system.

title: I Am Artemis: Dan Florez
summary: Dan Florez, test director for NASA's Exploration Ground Systems Program, describes witnessing the Artemis I launch from Kennedy Space Center's Launch Control Center roof at 1:47 a.m. EST on November 16, 2022.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Roll out for Static Fire Testing
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have been rolled out for static fire testing as part of the Artemis program. This follows the successful return of Orion.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus NG-23 mission completion.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has debuted two new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing capabilities and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making refinements at its Starbase Pad 2 in preparation for the return of Booster 19. The updates are part of ongoing efforts to enhance launch infrastructure at the facility.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II set a new record by conducting a successful lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft. The mission, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen, marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station (ISS), while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled for launch from California. These launches occur amid the ongoing Artemis II mission.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in a critical mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks the first crewed mission in the Artemis program.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads toward the Moon for the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned twice in the provided news snippet, but no specific details or context about the space industry news is given.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's significant milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is open for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency’s technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses a new technology or method for spacecraft orientation or pointing systems. It highlights advancements in precision pointing for space applications.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team tested ultralight graphene aerogels with lasers during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025, observing significant propulsion in microgravity. The study, led by Université Libre de Bruxelles and Khalifa University, demonstrated that light propulsion could be controlled by tuning the laser, with potential applications for solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Lasers and graphene could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated this potential.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space industry applications. The chip's versatility could reduce costs and improve efficiency in satellite and spacecraft systems.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and has 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programs.

title: Atomic-6 Launches Orbital Data Center Marketplace
summary: Atomic-6, a composite manufacturer based in Georgia, has launched ODC.Space, a marketplace to simplify securing orbital compute resources. This initiative aims to accelerate the growth of the orbital data center market.

title: Sophia Space and Kepler Sign Agreement to Demo ODC Tech
summary: Sophia Space and Kepler Communications have signed an agreement to demonstrate orbital-data-center (ODC) technology in space. This marks Sophia Space's first opportunity to validate its ODC software in a real-world space environment.

title: Hawkeye 360 Files For IPO
summary: Hawkeye 360, a space-based RF data and analytics firm, has filed for an IPO to raise capital as it expands in the defense-focused space sector. This move follows a trend of similar companies going public to support industry growth.

title: Albedo Ratchets Up the Power for its Second VLEO Flight
summary: Albedo unveiled a new VLEO-capable satellite bus designed for its second orbital mission, scheduled for 2027. The company aims to enhance power capabilities for this upcoming flight.

title: Moog’s “Tip to Tail” Contributions to the Artemis II Flight
summary: Moog has supplied over 100 components for the Artemis II mission, covering hardware from the rocket to the Orion spacecraft. This highlights Moog's extensive role in the upcoming lunar mission.

title: Artemis II Gives Airbus Hope For European Spaceflight
summary: Artemis II features the European Service Module (ESM), built by Airbus Defence and Space, marking a significant step for European involvement in spaceflight. This collaboration highlights Airbus's role in NASA's lunar mission.

title: Portal Closes $50M Series A
summary: Portal has closed a $50 million Series A funding round led by Geodesic Capital and Mach33, with additional investments from Booz Allen Ventures, Ark Invest, AlleyCorp, and FUSE.

title: The State of Launch 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's VP of launch discusses the competitive pressures in the space industry, highlighting how deadlines and capability demonstrations can determine winners and losers in launch contracts.

title: Vantor Unveils New Sat Classes: Vantage and Pulse
summary: Vantor introduced two new satellite classes, Vantage and Pulse, aimed at enhancing imagery resolution and revisit rate. The announcement highlights the company's focus on advancing its satellite technology.

title: The State of ISAM 2026
summary: The article discusses the growing complexity of In-Space Servicing, Assembly, and Manufacturing (ISAM) by 2026, noting that it can no longer be treated as a single concept. It highlights the expanding scope and challenges of ISAM as a key topic in the space industry.

title: MDA Announces Orbital Servicing Platform
summary: MDA Space unveiled MDA Midnight, an on-orbit servicing platform for rendezvous and proximity operations (RPO), at the Space Symposium. The platform is designed to detect, identify, and counter potential threats in space.

title: Darktrace’s Research Shows New Chinese Modus Operandi
summary: Darktrace released a research report titled 'Crimson Echo' analyzing evolving Chinese-nexus cyber threats. The report examines new modus operandi in cyber tradecraft through behavioral analysis.

title: Anthropic Launches Project Glasswing for Cybersecurity
summary: Anthropic has launched Project Glasswing, a cybersecurity initiative aimed at securing critical software. The project is being developed in collaboration with Amazon Web Services.

title: Top Space Cyber Execs Talk Increased Iranian Cyber Attacks
summary: Space companies are experiencing a rise in cyber attacks, particularly from Iran. Top cybersecurity executives from Vantor, SES, Viasat, and Telesat discussed these growing threats.

title: NSA Reveals Details of New LEO Security Report
summary: The NSA and other organizations released a new Cybersecurity Information Sheet (CSI) highlighting cybersecurity risks and mitigation strategies for Low Earth Orbit (LEO) systems. The report aims to address high-level security concerns in the space industry.

title: Artemis II Crew Completes Historic Lunar Flyby Mission
summary: The Artemis II crew successfully completed a historic lunar flyby mission and splashed down in the Pacific Ocean off San Diego after nearly 10 days. This marked NASA's first crewed mission beyond low Earth orbit since the Apollo era.

title: Emirates Connectivity Exec Explains the Airline’s Move to Starlink
summary: Emirates announced its adoption of Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi across its entire fleet, marking a significant shift in the airline's connectivity services. The deployment began in November of the previous year.

title: Amazon CEO Targets Mid-2026 Launch for Amazon Leo Service
summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announced that Amazon's satellite constellation service, Amazon Leo, is planned to launch in mid-2026. The exact launch date was not specified in the letter to shareholders.

title: BryceTech Report Shows SpaceX Accounted for 50% of Launches in 2025
summary: BryceTech's 2025 Year in Review reveals SpaceX dominated the launch market with 50% of all launches. The report highlights a shift toward small satellite deployments.

title: EnduroSat and Shield Space Strike European Partnership for Defense Missions
summary: EnduroSat and Shield Space have formed a strategic partnership to jointly operate space defense missions in Europe. The collaboration aims to leverage their combined technologies for on-orbit operations.
